subject: Fixed concurrency issue in PNF uploading
commit_message:
Fixed concurrency issue in PNF uploading

Signed-off-by: Francis Toth <francis.toth@yoppworks.com>
Change-Id: I89b77fd000219bded08e4566b585533dfca1b7e2
Issue-ID: AAI-3100

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-resources-master-verify-java/383/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-resources-master-verify-java/383

Patch Set 1:

Hi,

This change intents to fix the concurrency issue mentioned in AAI-300. A LockMap class has been added to deal with locks in a transactional fashion. This fix also leverages VAVR's implementation of Try.

Thank you in advance for your help,

Francis.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> The lock being proposed here doesn't solve the underlying issue. Due to the lock being set at this part of the code, there are many scenarios where duplicates can still be created even in an single instance of the resources microservice.
> For example, consider the following scenario where an same p-interface was created two different ways under an existing pserver:
> 
> User 1
> 
> PUT /aai/v12/cloud-infrastructure/pservers/pserver/test-pserver1
> 
> {
>   "hostname": "test-pserver1",
>   "p-interfaces": {
>     "p-interface": [
>       {
>         "interface-name": "test-interface1"
>       }
>     ]
>   }
> }
> 
> User 2
> 
> PUT /aai/v12/cloud-infrastructure/pservers/pserver/test-pserver1/p-interfaces/p-interface/test-interface1
> 
> {
>   "interface-name": "test-interface1"
> }
> 
> Also, we have an bulk single transaction and other bulk apis where they can create the objects and could result in creating duplicates.
> An better way to solve this problem that could also scale well with multiple instances of resources would be to use the locks that janusgraph provides at schema level.
> 
> https://docs.janusgraph.org/advanced-topics/eventual-consistency/
> 
> Updating the following code here where we are creating the schema and set the lock for aai-uri :
> 
> https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=aai/aai-common.git;a=blob;f=aai-core/src/main/java/org/onap/aai/dbgen/SchemaGenerator.java;h=af81c2d2956cf93b7cc50c8c12af78904ed0d656;hb=HEAD
> 
> mgmt.setConsistency(name, ConsistencyModifier.LOCK) // Ensures only one name per vertex
> mgmt.setConsistency(index, ConsistencyModifier.LOCK) // Ensures name uniqueness in the graph
> 
> From <https://docs.janusgraph.org/advanced-topics/eventual-consistency/> 
> 
> This would have an additional benefit of potentially being more consistent across many instances and not creating duplicates.
> Another point I would like to bring to attention as the solution proposed here is not configurable. If in future we found that this solution doesn't meet our performance criteria, we would like the ability to disable it.


This fix was tested as per below:

Fix tested in release: Frankfurt
Test case: create pnf via rest call http://{{onap}}:{{aai-port}}/aai/v14/network/pnfs/pnf/fra-qa-pnf-concurrency-fix-test-2
Status: failed (see below)
 
During local testing, it was discovered that execution path does not involve SchemaGenerator.java class and thus fix did NOT get executed and issue was not resolved.
Execution path:
LegacyMoxyConsumer::handleWrites() --> HttpEntry::process() -->DBSerializer::createNewVertex() and ::serializeToDb ()
It uses the JanusGraph library.

Abandoned

done a different way, please raise again if still needed
